commit.message:
Ship libbearssl.a in Linux release bundles for TLS support

Release CI now builds BearSSL from the submodule during Linux
packaging (293 .c files → libbearssl.a ~2-3MB) and injects it into
the tarball at toolchain/lib/libbearssl.a.

find_bearssl_lib() now searches:
1. deps/laststanding/bearssl/build/ (dev mode)
2. <exe-dir>/toolchain/lib/ (release bundle)
3. <exe-dir>/lib/ (minimal install)

This makes TLS work out-of-the-box on Linux after installing from
the release tarball. Windows uses SChannel (no BearSSL needed).

- If set_permissions(0o600) fails after fs::write, the secrets file was
  previously left world-readable permanently. Now both files are removed
  consistently, matching the cleanup at L:329-330 and L:343-344.

Also adds third council recommendation file.

Docs versioning: Add glob support, better validation and reporting (#3278)

* Docs versioning: Add glob support, better validation and reporting

Normalize CLI target specs (handle Windows/backslashes, ./, absolute paths) and classify them as file/dir/glob. Implement matching logic and validation (report matched files and unmatched selectors, return rc=2 on unmatched), and apply target specs in scan/update. Add helpers (normalize_target_spec, compile_target_specs, validate_requested_targets, print_target_match_summary, iter_scan_candidate_paths) and adjust main argument help. Update tests to cover directory, glob, Windows-style paths, and CLI reporting.

* Handle invalid target specs in validation

Treat target specs that fail normalization as kind "invalid" so they are not silently ignored. compile_target_specs now appends invalid specs, target_spec_matches_path returns False for invalid kinds, and validate_requested_targets records invalid raw selectors as unmatched (and safely iterates when specs may be None). This ensures malformed or non-normalizable CLI selectors are reported back to the user rather than dropped.

* tests: add repo/cfg fixtures and refactor tests

Introduce shared pytest fixtures (repo, cfg) and import Callable to reduce repetition of tmp_path/git init logic across tests. Refactor many tests to use the new fixtures and ToolConfig factory, and add/adjust tests covering target validation and scanning edge cases (several validate_requested_targets variations, scan_files with invalid only-targets, and main returning 2 for invalid selector). Overall this centralizes repo/config setup and adds coverage for target handling behavior.

* Clarify --targets help text

Expand and clarify the --targets argument help strings in tools/docs_and_notebooks_check.py for the update and normalize subcommands. The updated messages document that --targets accepts exact files, directories, and glob patterns (with examples) and note that both '/' and '\\' path separators are accepted. This is a documentation-only change to improve user guidance; no functional behavior is altered.

commit.message:
Feat: JSON editor for sensor/asset/account attributes (#2093)

* fix: click context issue for an apparently different local way of running the web server

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* dev: tech spike on JSON editor for sensor attributes

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* feat: db migration for account attributes

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* dev: first implementation of JSON editor for sensors, assets and accounts

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* dev: fix opening JSON editor on asset page, and try theming

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui: switch JSON attributes editor from josdejong/jsoneditor to @json-editor/json-editor
Context:
- Previous implementation used josdejong/jsoneditor@9.10.0 with Tree+Code modes
  backed by Ace editor, which caused caret desync and hard-to-style menu items
- User asked to switch to @json-editor/json-editor for Bootstrap-native theming
Change:
- base.html: replace josdejong CDN (+ CSS) with @json-editor/json-editor@2.15.2
- _macros.html: rewrite modal to use @json-editor/json-editor for the Form tab
  and a plain Bootstrap textarea for the JSON tab (no Ace dependency, no caret issues)
- Modal now resets to Form tab on every open and syncs both views on tab switch
- config_defaults.py: default theme renamed from 'default' to 'bootstrap5'
  (the actual @json-editor/json-editor theme identifier)
- flexmeasures.css: replace josdejong-specific rules (.jsoneditor-menu, dark/github
  overrides) with .fm-json-textarea and .fm-je-container helpers
- documentation/configuration.rst: update FLEXMEASURES_JSONEDITOR_THEME to list
  the @json-editor/json-editor themes (bootstrap5/4/3, tailwind, spectre, barebones, html)
Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: fix five styling issues found during testing
Context:
- Tested the @json-editor/json-editor modal on sensor, asset and account pages
Issues fixed:
1. JSON textarea too small (~2.5 lines visible)
   → add rows='20' to <textarea> and explicit height: 420px in CSS
2. Checkmark overlaps selected text in the type dropdown
   → Bootstrap adds .is-valid to all selects (schema is open, everything
     validates) which injects a checkmark background-image alongside the
     arrow, causing overlap; override restores the plain arrow-only image
3. 'root' label appears as the form header
   → title: '' is falsy in JS so the library falls back to the path key
     'root'; use title: '\u200b' (zero-width space, truthy) instead
4. 'Properties' button has rounded corners only on the right side
   → the button sits in a .btn-group whose sibling buttons are present in
     the DOM but hidden (collapse / edit-JSON disabled via options);
     Bootstrap's :not(:last-child) rule flattens corners of the first
     visible button; fix by querying visible children after init and
     restoring full border-radius when only one is visible
5. Array button labels show ' item' / ' last' (missing verb) and
   'Delete all' is completely invisible
   → iconlib: 'fontawesome5' was set but only Font Awesome 4.7 is loaded;
     FA5 class names (fas fa-*) produce invisible icons, and 'Delete all'
     has no text fallback so the button has zero visible content;
     change iconlib to 'fontawesome4' to match the loaded library
Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* style: switch to spectre as the default theme

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* feat: do not disable buttons to Edit JSON

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* feat: do not disable collapse buttons

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* style: prefer toast over alert

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: block tab switch when JSON tab contains invalid JSON
Use show.bs.tab (fires before Bootstrap moves focus) to call
event.preventDefault() when switching JSON → Form and the textarea
does not contain valid JSON.  The tab stays on JSON and a toast explains
why.  shown.bs.tab (fires after the switch succeeds) now handles only the
happy path and no longer needs a try/catch error branch for this case.
Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* style: relabel button to edit custom attributes (we are already within a sidepanel specifically for editing)

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: replace raw attributes text field with 'Open editor' button
Asset page:
- Remove the asset_form.attributes text input (label + description + field +
  error list) from the edit side-panel; it was a raw JSON textarea that
  required manual editing and duplicated the new modal editor
- Keep only the Open editor button that triggers the modal
Sensor and account pages:
- Already had no raw attributes field; rename button label from
  'Edit attributes' to 'Open editor' for consistency
Tests updated to match the new button label.
Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: restore attributes label and description above Open editor button
The previous commit removed the entire attributes form-group.  Keep the
WTForms label and description text so the field retains its context in
the side-panel; only the raw <input> (and its error list) is replaced by
the Open editor button.
Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: add attributes label and description to sensor and account pages
Context:
- Asset page already showed the WTForms label and description above the Open
  editor button; sensor and account pages showed the button with no context
Change:
- Sensor side-panel: add label 'Other attributes (JSON)' and description text
  above the Open editor button, matching the asset page layout
- Account side-panel: same addition

* ui/json-editor: define attributes field label and description once server-side
Context:
- Label and description strings were duplicated across three pages (asset,
  sensor, account); any wording change required three edits
Change:
- forms.py: extract ATTRIBUTES_FIELD_LABEL / ATTRIBUTES_FIELD_DESCRIPTION
  module-level constants; AssetForm.attributes now references them
- _macros.html: json_attributes_editor_button gains optional field_label and
  field_description params; when supplied they are rendered as a <label> and
  <small> above the button, removing the need for per-page HTML
- asset_properties.html, sensors/index.html, accounts/account.html: pass the
  constants through the macro instead of hardcoding the strings
- assets/views.py, sensors.py, accounts.py: import the constants and pass them
  to the template as attributes_label / attributes_description

* ui/json-editor: fix btn-group border-radius when .je-modal is a sibling
Context:
- @json-editor/json-editor places a div.je-modal inside .btn-group next to
  the action buttons (Properties / EditJSON / Collapse)
- Bootstrap's :not(:last-child) strips right-radius from the last button
  when .je-modal trails the group; :not(.btn-check)+.btn strips left-radius
  from any button that directly follows .je-modal
- This caused the 'JSON' (Edit-JSON) button to show no left border-radius
Change:
- Add two CSS overrides scoped to .fm-je-container .btn-group:
    1. :has(> div.je-modal) > button:last-of-type  → restore right-radius
    2. div.je-modal + button                        → restore left-radius

* ui/json-editor: open Edit Attributes modal with more height
Context:
- Modal opened to just the height of its content, which was often short on
  first open before the json-editor had rendered its fields
Change:
- Add class fm-json-modal to the outer modal div in the macro
- Add CSS: .fm-json-modal .modal-content { min-height: 70vh } so the dialog
  occupies a generous portion of the viewport from the moment it opens

* ui/json-editor: widen inline JSON editing panel to full modal width
Context:
- Clicking the 'Edit JSON' button inside the form editor opens a div.je-modal
  panel with a textarea and Save / Copy / Cancel buttons
- The panel was width-constrained, wrapping the footer buttons to a second row
- The free-text textarea could only be resized vertically in the UI, not
  horizontally, leaving unused space
Change:
- .fm-je-container .je-modal: set width / max-width to 100% so the panel
  spans the full container
- .fm-je-container .je-modal textarea: same, ensuring the field fills the
  available width rather than leaving empty space to the right

* ui/json-editor: add copy buttons for array items
Context:
- Array items in the Form tab only had move-up / move-down / delete buttons;
  there was no way to duplicate an item without manually re-typing it
Change:
- Pass enable_array_copy: true to the JSONEditor constructor; @json-editor/
  json-editor then renders a Copy button next to each array item so users can
  clone an existing entry as a starting point for a new one

* style: fix button radius

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: widen je-modal to 150% so action buttons fit one line
Context:
- .je-modal was set to width: 100% of its .fm-je-container parent
- With narrower modals the Save / Copy / Cancel footer buttons wrapped
  to a second row, wasting vertical space
Change:
- Raise width and max-width to 150% so the inline JSON panel is wider
  than its container, giving the three footer buttons enough horizontal
  room to sit on a single line

* ui/json-editor: move attributes editor button above Save on sensor and account pages
Context:
- On both the sensor side-panel and the account side-panel the Open editor
  button was rendered below the form's Save button (outside the form element,
  with padding-top spacer)
- Users expected the attributes field to appear as part of the form, above
  the Save action, consistent with the asset page layout
Change:
- sensors/index.html: remove the post-form <div> wrapper; place the button
  inside the <form> in a .form-group above the Save button
- accounts/account.html: same restructuring; remove the 60 px padding-top
  spacer div and render the button in a .form-group before the Save button

* ui/json-editor: fix attributes description color to use --gray
Context:
- The field_description in the json_attributes_editor_button macro was
  rendered with Bootstrap's 'text-muted' class, which resolves to
  var(--bs-secondary-color) — a different shade than the rest of the
  FlexMeasures UI uses for secondary text
Change:
- Replace class="text-muted" with an inline style="color: var(--gray)"
  so the description matches the project-wide --gray custom property
  (#bbb, defined in base.html)

* ui/json-editor: fix je-switcher padding so dropdown arrow doesn't overlap text
Context:
- The type-switcher <select class="je-switcher"> had no padding-right, so
  the native dropdown chevron rendered on top of the selected option text
Change:
- Add .fm-je-container .je-switcher { padding: 0px 50px 0px 3px }
  The 50 px right-padding keeps the text clear of the arrow; 3 px left
  gives a small inset to align with the rest of the form fields

* Revert "ui/json-editor: add copy buttons for array items"

This reverts commit 23f79d58f058d32c9a8af7527330268b35d1d3fc.

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: add copy buttons for array items
Context:
- Array items in the Form tab only had move-up / move-down / delete buttons;
  there was no way to duplicate an item without manually re-typing it
Change:
- Pass enable_array_copy: true to the JSONEditor constructor; @json-editor/
  json-editor then renders a Copy button next to each array item so users can
  clone an existing entry as a starting point for a new one

* ui/json-editor: fix array copy to insert at index+1 instead of end
Context:
- enable_array_copy: true tells @json-editor/json-editor to render Copy
  buttons for array items (commit 23f79d58)
- The library's built-in createCopyButton handler calls Array.push() to
  add the clone, so copies always land at the END of the array regardless
  of which item the user copied
Change:
- Monkey-patch JSONEditor.defaults.editors.array.prototype.createCopyButton
  before instantiation with a replacement that uses Array.splice(idx+1, 0, copy)
  so the clone is inserted immediately after the source row
- A guard flag (_fmCopyPatched) ensures the prototype is patched only once
  even when multiple editor modals are present on the same page
- refreshValue / onChange / trigger('copyRow') calls are preserved to keep
  the editor's internal state consistent with the original implementation

* ui/json-editor: fix array copy via capture-phase intercept
Context:
- Previous fix targeted prototype.createCopyButton (no underscore) which
  does not exist; the real method is _createCopyButton so the patch was
  a no-op and the original broken handler kept running
- The library's _createCopyButton accesses schema.items.type without
  null-guarding schema.items; when arrays come from additionalProperties: {}
  items is undefined, causing:
    TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'type')
- This bug exists in both array editor implementations in the library
  (regular and table-array variants)
Change:
- Remove the ineffective prototype patch
- Register a capture-phase addEventListener on _editorEl (the json-editor
  container) that intercepts all .json-editor-btntype-copy clicks
- Capture phase fires BEFORE the button's own bubble-phase handler;
  evt.stopPropagation() prevents the library's broken handler from running
- Our handler uses data-schemapath (set by json-editor on every container)
  + _jeInstance.getEditor(path) to find the correct array editor instance,
  then splices a deep clone at index+1 — works for any nesting depth and
  both array editor types, even for buttons created after setValue() calls

* ui/json-editor: fix array copy — walk up DOM to find array editor
Context:
- Previous fix used btn.closest('[data-schemapath]') which returns the
  *item* editor container (e.g. root.myarray.0), not the *array* editor
  container (e.g. root.myarray)
- getEditor('root.myarray.0').getValue() returns the item value (object /
  string), not an array, so Array.isArray() returned false and the handler
  silently bailed out — copy button appeared to do nothing
Change:
- Replace closest() with a while-loop that walks up through all ancestors
  with data-schemapath, calling getEditor(path).getValue() on each until
  it finds one that returns an Array — that is the correct array editor
- Remove the now-redundant Array.isArray guard (the loop already ensures
  arrayEditor's value is an array before we reach the splice)

* ui/json-editor: move version to FLEXMEASURES_JS_VERSIONS config
Context:
- The @json-editor/json-editor CDN URL had the version hardcoded as
  '2.15.2' while all other JS libraries (vega, leaflet, …) are versioned
  via the FLEXMEASURES_JS_VERSIONS dict in config_defaults.py and
  referenced in templates as {{ js_versions.<key> }}
Change:
- config_defaults.py: add jsoneditor="2.15.2" to FLEXMEASURES_JS_VERSIONS
- base.html: replace the hardcoded version with {{ js_versions.jsoneditor }}

* fix: security update for cryptography to v46.0.7 minimum

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

* ui/json-editor: read version via config not js_versions in base.html
Context:
- js_versions is only injected by render_flexmeasures_template (used for
  logged-in pages); Flask-Security's login page renders base.html via the

Change:
- Use config.FLEXMEASURES_JS_VERSIONS.jsoneditor instead; Flask always
  provides 'config' in every template context regardless of render path

* docs: changelog entry

Signed-off-by: F.N. Claessen <claessen@seita.nl>

Complete enterprise-grade frontend with:

feat: FLAC passthrough + pre-buffer for reduced playback latency

- Eliminate local ffmpeg transcoding: raw audio bytes (FLAC/MP3/AAC)
  pass directly from Yandex CDN to MA's ffmpeg
- Pre-buffer system: audio download starts on Ynison track change
  before the player HTTP GET, hiding API/CDN latency
- PluginSource audio_format changed from PCM_S16LE to FLAC
- Seek uses ffmpeg fallback only when needed
- Reduces playback start delay by ~3-5s (15-20s → 10-12s)
- 80 tests passing, 9 new tests for passthrough and pre-buffer

chore(tooling): Split publish into validate + sdk + extension jobs

Validation (typecheck, tests, build) is separated from publishing to ensure
that only verified artifacts are published. Splitting SDK and extension
publishing into distinct jobs makes failures clearer, allows different
publish-time configuration (e.g., avoiding caches to ensure clean builds),
and enables generating per-package SBOMs for traceability. This improves
supply-chain hygiene and auditing without changing overall release semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(tooling): Add YAML schema hint to workflows

Adding the yaml-language-server schema header enables in-editor schema
validation and autocompletion for GitHub Actions workflow files. This
improves developer productivity, reduces misconfigurations, and makes
workflow files easier to author and review. The change is non-invasive and
improves the DX for contributors.
